                },
                ("actions[0].destination", "string"),
                id="destination-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "a",
                            "destination": "~/a",
                            "hidden": "yes",
                        }
                    ],
                },
                ("actions[0].hidden", "boolean"),
                id="hidden-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "a",
                            "destination": "~/a",
                            "elevated": "yes",
                        }
                    ],
                },
                ("actions[0].elevated", "boolean"),
                id="elevated-type",
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, object],
        expected_error_parts: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "git" / "config.yaml", config_payload)

            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",

                    "actions": [{"type": "run", "source": "/etc/passwd"}],

                    "version": "1",

                id="destination-string",

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, object],
        expected_error_parts: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:

                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "move", "source": "file.sh"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].type", "move"),
                id="action-type-unknown",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "actions": [{"type": "run"}]},
                ("actions[0].source", "required"),
                id="action-source-required",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "run", "source": 1}],
                },
                ("actions[0].source", "string"),
                id="action-source-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "run", "source": ""}],

                ("actions[0].source", "non-empty"),
                id="action-source-empty",
            ),
            pytest.param(

                    "version": "1",

                    "actions": [{"type": "run", "source": "/etc/passwd"}],

                ("actions[0].source", "module-relative"),
                id="action-source-relative",

                id="destination-required-link",

            pytest.param(

                    "version": "1",

                    "version": "1",

                id="destination-string",

                            "type": "copy",

                            "destination": "~/a",
                            "hidden": "yes",

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,

        expected_error_parts: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:

                id="actions-type",

                id="action-type-string",
            ),

                    "actions": [{"type": "move", "source": "file.sh"}],

                ("actions[0].type", "move"),

                id="action-source-empty",
            ),

                id="action-source-relative",

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, object],
        expected_error_parts: tuple[str, ...],

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields(

        tmp_path: Path,

        expected_error_parts: tuple[str, ...],

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_wildcard_appends_filename(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        home_dir = tmp_path / "home"
        home_dir.mkdir()
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "dotfiles",
            configs={
                "config.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "*",
                            "destination": "~/cfg/",
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={"z.txt": "Z", "a.txt": "A"},
        )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, home_dir=home_dir)
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)
        actions = payload["modules"][0]["actions"]
>       assert [Path(action["source"]).name for action in actions] == [
            "a.txt",
            "z.txt",
        ]
E       AssertionError: assert ['*'] == ['a.txt', 'z.txt']
E         
E         At index 0 diff: '*' != 'a.txt'
E         Right contains one more item: 'z.txt'
E         
E         Full diff:
E           [
E         +     '*',
E         -     'a.txt',
E         -     'z.txt',
E           ]

tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_plan_preserves_file_actio0')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_preserves_file_action_order(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        home_dir = tmp_path / "home"
        home_dir.mkdir()
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "shell",
            configs={
                "config.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "a.txt",
                            "destination": "~/a.txt",
                        },
                        {"type": "run", "source": "setup.sh"},
                        {
                            "type": "link",
                            "source": "b.txt",
                            "destination": "~/b.txt",
                        },
                    ]
                )

            configs={
                "config.yaml": module_schema(
                    os_name="linux",
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "missing.txt",
                            "destination": "~/missing.txt",
                        }
                    ],
                )
            },
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "good",
            configs={"config.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
    
        filtered_by_os = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--os", "macos")
        assert filtered_by_os.returncode == 0, filtered_by_os.stderr
        assert parse_stdout_json(filtered_by_os)["modules"] == [
            {"name": "good", "actions": []}
        ]
    
        filtered_by_module = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--module", "good")
        assert filtered_by_module.returncode == 0, filtered_by_module.stderr
        assert parse_stdout_json(filtered_by_module)["modules"] == [
            {"name": "good", "actions": []}
        ]
    
        included = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--module", "bad_linux")

            ),
        )

            pytest.param(("--os", "macos"), True, id="macos"),
            pytest.param(("--os", "linux"), False, id="linux"),
        ],
    )
    def test_plan_filters_dock_by_os_flag(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        extra_args: tuple[str, ...],
        expect_dock: bool,
    ) -> None:
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "dock" / "dock.yaml",
            dock_schema(["Launchpad", "Safari", "Firefox"]),
        )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, *extra_args)
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)
        if not expect_dock:

            return

                        "value_type": "float",

                    },
                ]
            ),
        )

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

        entrypoint_argv,

        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_plan_deduplicates_deps0')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_deduplicates_deps(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Deduplicates repeated dependencies before ordering modules."""
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "app",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[],
                    depends_on=["core", "core", "core"],
                )
            },
        )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)
>       assert [module["name"] for module in payload["modules"]] == [
            "core",
            "app",
        ]

E         At index 0 diff: 'app' != 'core'
E         
E         Full diff:
E           [
E         +     'app',
E               'core',
E         -     'app',
E           ]

                id="environments-valid-depends-on",

                        }

                )
            },
        )

            tmp_path,
            "b",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["c"])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "c",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["a"])},
        )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

.evaluation_tests/test_checkpoint_4.py:573: AssertionError
__________________ test_plan_profile_dedup_inheritance_chain ___________________

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_plan_profile_dedup_inheri0')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_profile_dedup_inheritance_chain(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Plan deduplicates inherited modules across transitive profile hierarchy."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema(
                {
                    "base": {"modules": ["core", "dev-tools"]},
                    "work": {
                        "extends": "base",
                        "modules": ["dev-tools", "work-apps"],
                    },
                    "full": {"extends": "work", "modules": ["core", "media"]},
                }
            ),
        )
        for module_name in ["core", "dev-tools", "work-apps", "media"]:
            write_module(
                tmp_path,
                module_name,
                configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
            )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--profile", "full")
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)
>       assert _module_names(payload) == [
            "core",
            "dev-tools",
            "work-apps",
            "media",
        ]
E       AssertionError: assert ['core', 'dev-tools', 'media', 'work-apps'] == ['core', 'dev-tools', 'work-apps', 'media']
E         
E         At index 2 diff: 'media' != 'work-apps'
E         
E         Full diff:
E           [
E               'core',
E               'dev-tools',
E         +     'media',
E               'work-apps',
E         -     'media',
E           ]

.evaluation_tests/test_checkpoint_4.py:609: AssertionError
___________________ test_plan_profile_transitive_inheritance ___________________

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_plan_profile_transitive_i0')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_profile_transitive_inheritance(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Plan resolves transitive profile inheritance before ordering modules."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema(
                {
                    "base": {"modules": ["core"]},

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_build_profile_name_collisions_do_not_overwrite_other_profile_outputs(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Build for one profile does not overwrite unrelated profile artifacts."""
        output_dir = tmp_path / "out"
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema(
                {
                    "a-b": {"modules": ["alpha"]},
                    "a/b": {"modules": ["beta"]},
                }
            ),
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "alpha",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "alpha.txt",
                            "destination": "/tmp/alpha.txt",
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={"alpha.txt": "alpha"},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "beta",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "beta.txt",
                            "destination": "/tmp/beta.txt",
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={"beta.txt": "beta"},
        )
    
        first_result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv,
            tmp_path,
            "--os",
            "macos",
            "--output",
            "out",
            "--release",
        )
    
        assert first_result.returncode == 0, first_result.stderr
        first_payload = parse_stdout_json(first_result)
        alpha_package = _package_for_profile(first_payload, "a-b")
        alpha_dir = _resolve_reported_path(tmp_path, alpha_package["directory"])
        alpha_archive = _resolve_reported_path(tmp_path, alpha_package["archive"])
        assert (alpha_dir / "alpha" / "alpha.txt").read_text() == "alpha"
        assert not (alpha_dir / "beta" / "beta.txt").exists()
    
        second_result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv,
            tmp_path,
            "--os",
            "macos",
            "--profile",
            "a/b",
            "--output",
            "out",
            "--release",
        )
    
        assert second_result.returncode == 0, second_result.stderr
        assert (alpha_dir / "alpha" / "alpha.txt").read_text() == "alpha"
        assert not (alpha_dir / "beta" / "beta.txt").exists()

.evaluation_tests/test_checkpoint_5.py:482: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

filename = '/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_build_profile_name_collis0/out/a-b-package.archive'
extract_dir = '/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_build_profile_name_collis0/alpha-release'
format = None

    def unpack_archive(filename, extract_dir=None, format=None, *, filter=None):
        """Unpack an archive.
    
        `filename` is the name of the archive.
    
        `extract_dir` is the name of the target directory, where the archive
        is unpacked. If not provided, the current working directory is used.
    
        `format` is the archive format: one of "zip", "tar", "gztar", "bztar",
        or "xztar".  Or any other registered format.  If not provided,
        unpack_archive will use the filename extension and see if an unpacker
        was registered for that extension.
    
        In case none is found, a ValueError is raised.
    
        If `filter` is given, it is passed to the underlying
        extraction function.
        """
        sys.audit("shutil.unpack_archive", filename, extract_dir, format)
    
        if extract_dir is None:
            extract_dir = os.getcwd()
    
        extract_dir = os.fspath(extract_dir)
        filename = os.fspath(filename)
    
        if filter is None:
            filter_kwargs = {}
        else:
            filter_kwargs = {'filter': filter}
        if format is not None:
            try:
                format_info = _UNPACK_FORMATS[format]
            except KeyError:
                raise ValueError("Unknown unpack format '{0}'".format(format)) from None
    
            func = format_info[1]
            func(filename, extract_dir, **dict(format_info[2]), **filter_kwargs)
        else:
            # we need to look at the registered unpackers supported extensions
            format = _find_unpack_format(filename)
            if format is None:
>               raise ReadError("Unknown archive format '{0}'".format(filename))

tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_build_copies_referenced_d2')
file_name = 'data.yml', content = 'name: cfg\n'

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        ("file_name", "content"),
        [
            pytest.param("data.json", '{"name":"cfg"}\n', id="json"),
            pytest.param("data.yaml", "name: cfg\n", id="yaml"),
            pytest.param("data.yml", "name: cfg\n", id="yml"),
        ],
    )
    def test_build_copies_referenced_data_files_even_with_config_like_extensions(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        file_name: str,
        content: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Build copies referenced JSON/YAML data files that actions reference."""
        destination = tmp_path / "dest" / file_name
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": file_name,
                            "destination": str(destination),
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={file_name: content},
        )
    
        result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--os", "macos", "--output", "out"
        )
    
>       assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_install_script_dry_run_outputs_all_action_types_in_plan_order(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Dry-run installer prints all selected actions in required output format."""
        home_dir = tmp_path / "home"
        home_dir.mkdir()
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema({"work": {"modules": ["core"]}}),
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    os_name="macos",
                    package_manager="brew",
                    packages=["coreutils"],
                    applications=["firefox"],
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "link",
                            "source": "editorconfig",
                            "destination": "~/.editorconfig",
                        },
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "logo.txt",
                            "destination": "~/logo.txt",
                        },
                        {
                            "type": "run",
                            "source": "setup.sh",
                        },
                    ],
                ),
                "preferences.yaml": preferences_schema(
                    [
                        {
                            "name": "Disable auto-rearrange",
                            "domain": "com.apple.dock",
                            "key": "mru-spaces",
                            "value": False,
                            "value_type": "bool",
                            "apply_command": (
                                "defaults write com.apple.dock "
                                "mru-spaces -bool false"
                            ),
                        }
                    ]
                ),
                "dock.yaml": dock_schema(["Launchpad", "Safari", "Firefox"]),
                "environments.yaml": environments_schema(
                    [
                        {
                            "language": "python",
                            "versions": ["3.11.2"],
                            "manager": {
                                "name": "pyenv",
                                "plugins": [
                                    {
                                        "name": "pyenv-virtualenv",
                                        "virtual_environments": [
                                            {
                                                "version": "3.11.2",
                                                "name": "default-3.11",
                                            }
                                        ],
                                    }
                                ],
                            },
                        }
                    ]
                ),
            },
            files={
                "editorconfig": "root=true",
                "logo.txt": "logo",
                "setup.sh": "echo setup",
            },
        )

            entrypoint_argv,
            tmp_path,
            "--os",
            "macos",
            "--profile",
            "work",
            "--output",
            "out",
            home_dir=home_dir,
        )

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_install_script_executes_p0')
monkeypatch = <_pytest.monkeypatch.MonkeyPatch object at 0x787f612591f0>
os_name = 'macos', package_manager = 'brew', package_name = 'coreutils'
application_name = 'firefox', allowed_prefixes = ('brew ',)

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        (
            "os_name",
            "package_manager",
            "package_name",
            "application_name",
            "allowed_prefixes",
        ),
        [
            pytest.param(
                "macos",
                "brew",
                "coreutils",
                "firefox",
                ("brew ",),
                id="brew",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                "linux",
                "apt",
                "curl",
                "vim",
                ("apt ", "apt-get "),
                id="apt",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                "linux",
                "yum",
                "wget",
                "htop",
                ("yum ",),
                id="yum",
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_install_script_executes_package_and_app_commands_for_each_manager(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
        os_name: str,
        package_manager: str,
        package_name: str,
        application_name: str,
        allowed_prefixes: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:
        """Installer executes package and application installation via OS manager commands."""
        log_path = tmp_path / "invocations.log"
        fake_bin = tmp_path / "bin"
        fake_bin.mkdir()
    
        for command_name in ["brew", "apt", "apt-get", "yum"]:
            command_path = fake_bin / command_name
            command_path.write_text(
                "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
                f'printf \'%s\\n\' "{command_name} $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            )
            command_path.chmod(0o755)
    
        sudo_cmd = fake_bin / "sudo"
        sudo_cmd.write_text(
            "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "sudo $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            'exec "$@"\n'
        )
        sudo_cmd.chmod(0o755)
    
        monkeypatch.setenv("RIG_INVOCATION_LOG", str(log_path))
        monkeypatch.setenv("PATH", f"{fake_bin}:{os.environ['PATH']}")
    
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    os_name=os_name,
                    package_manager=package_manager,
                    packages=[package_name],
                    applications=[application_name],
                    actions=[],
                )
            },
        )
    
        build_result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--os", os_name, "--output", "out"
        )
>       assert build_result.returncode == 0, build_result.stderr
E       AssertionError: {"error": "no_manifest", "details": "no manifest found but --profile was specified"}
E         
E       assert 1 == 0
E        +  where 1 = CompletedProcess(args=['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py', 'build', '/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_install_script_executes_p0', '--os', 'macos', '--output', 'out'], returncode=1, stdout='', stderr='{"error": "no_manifest", "details": "no manifest found but --profile was specified"}\n').returncode

os_name = 'linux', package_manager = 'yum', package_name = 'wget'
application_name = 'htop', allowed_prefixes = ('yum ',)

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        (
            "os_name",
            "package_manager",
            "package_name",
            "application_name",
            "allowed_prefixes",
        ),
        [
            pytest.param(
                "macos",
                "brew",
                "coreutils",
                "firefox",
                ("brew ",),
                id="brew",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                "linux",
                "apt",
                "curl",
                "vim",
                ("apt ", "apt-get "),
                id="apt",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                "linux",
                "yum",
                "wget",
                "htop",
                ("yum ",),
                id="yum",
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_install_script_executes_package_and_app_commands_for_each_manager(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
        os_name: str,
        package_manager: str,
        package_name: str,
        application_name: str,
        allowed_prefixes: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:
        """Installer executes package and application installation via OS manager commands."""
        log_path = tmp_path / "invocations.log"
        fake_bin = tmp_path / "bin"
        fake_bin.mkdir()
    
        for command_name in ["brew", "apt", "apt-get", "yum"]:
            command_path = fake_bin / command_name
            command_path.write_text(
                "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
                f'printf \'%s\\n\' "{command_name} $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            )
            command_path.chmod(0o755)
    
        sudo_cmd = fake_bin / "sudo"
        sudo_cmd.write_text(
            "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "sudo $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            'exec "$@"\n'
        )
        sudo_cmd.chmod(0o755)
    
        monkeypatch.setenv("RIG_INVOCATION_LOG", str(log_path))
        monkeypatch.setenv("PATH", f"{fake_bin}:{os.environ['PATH']}")
    
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    os_name=os_name,
                    package_manager=package_manager,
                    packages=[package_name],
                    applications=[application_name],
                    actions=[],
                )
            },
        )
    
        build_result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--os", os_name, "--output", "out"
        )
>       assert build_result.returncode == 0, build_result.stderr
E       AssertionError: {"error": "no_manifest", "details": "no manifest found but --profile was specified"}

format = None

    def unpack_archive(filename, extract_dir=None, format=None, *, filter=None):
        """Unpack an archive.
    
        `filename` is the name of the archive.
    
        `extract_dir` is the name of the target directory, where the archive
        is unpacked. If not provided, the current working directory is used.
    
        `format` is the archive format: one of "zip", "tar", "gztar", "bztar",
        or "xztar".  Or any other registered format.  If not provided,
        unpack_archive will use the filename extension and see if an unpacker
        was registered for that extension.
    
        In case none is found, a ValueError is raised.
    
        If `filter` is given, it is passed to the underlying
        extraction function.
        """
        sys.audit("shutil.unpack_archive", filename, extract_dir, format)
    
        if extract_dir is None:
            extract_dir = os.getcwd()
    
        extract_dir = os.fspath(extract_dir)
        filename = os.fspath(filename)
    
        if filter is None:
            filter_kwargs = {}
        else:
            filter_kwargs = {'filter': filter}
        if format is not None:
            try:
                format_info = _UNPACK_FORMATS[format]
            except KeyError:
                raise ValueError("Unknown unpack format '{0}'".format(format)) from None
    
            func = format_info[1]
            func(filename, extract_dir, **dict(format_info[2]), **filter_kwargs)
        else:
            # we need to look at the registered unpackers supported extensions
            format = _find_unpack_format(filename)
            if format is None:
>               raise ReadError("Unknown archive format '{0}'".format(filename))

            "  exit 88\n"
            "fi\n"
            'mkdir -p "$dest"\n'
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "git $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
        )
        git_path.chmod(0o755)

            "dev",

                                        ],
                                    }
                                ],
                            },
                        }

                )
            },
        )

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_install_script_dry_run_orders_actions_across_modules(

    ) -> None:

            "macos",

            "all",
            "--output",
            "out",

            tmp_path, parse_stdout_json(build_result)["packages"][0]["directory"]
        )
    
        dry_run = _run_install_dry_run(package_dir)
        assert dry_run.returncode == 0, dry_run.stderr

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_build_reports_file_not_found_before_conflict(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Missing source files produce file_not_found before destination conflicts."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema({"all": {"modules": ["a", "b"]}}),
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "a",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "missing.txt",
                            "destination": "/tmp/shared.txt",
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "b",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "link",
                            "source": "b.txt",
                            "destination": "/tmp/shared.txt",
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={"b.txt": "b"},
        )
    
        result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv,
            tmp_path,
            "--os",
            "macos",
            "--profile",
            "all",
        )

_____ test_build_reports_file_not_found_before_dependency_before_conflict ______

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_build_reports_file_not_fo1')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_build_reports_file_not_found_before_dependency_before_conflict(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """file_not_found takes precedence over dependency errors and destination conflicts."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema({"all": {"modules": ["a", "b", "app"]}}),
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "a",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "missing.txt",
                            "destination": "/tmp/shared.txt",
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "b",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "link",
                            "source": "b.txt",
                            "destination": "/tmp/shared.txt",
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={"b.txt": "b"},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "app",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["core"])},
        )
    
        result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv,
            tmp_path,
            "--os",
            "macos",
            "--profile",
            "all",
        )
    
        assert result.returncode == 1
        error = parse_stderr_json(result)
>       assert error["error"] == "file_not_found"
E       AssertionError: assert 'missing_dependency' == 'file_not_found'
E         
E         - file_not_found
E         + missing_dependency

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_build_resolves_multi_level_profile_inheritance(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Profile inheritance resolves through multiple extension levels."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema(
                {
                    "base": {"modules": ["core"]},
                    "intermediate": {"extends": "base", "modules": ["tools"]},
                    "full": {"extends": "intermediate", "modules": ["app"]},
                }
            ),
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "tools",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "app",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[],
                    depends_on=["core"],
                )
            },
        )
    
        result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv,
            tmp_path,
            "--os",
            "macos",
            "--profile",
            "full",
            "--output",
            "out",
        )
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)
